channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: WFH CHADS REPORT IN — 75 replies — 300.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
WFH CHADS REPORT IN

Open thread • Replies: 75 • Updated: 2026-03-10 19:35 UTC

OP

Let's all laugh at the wagies who have to pay insane amounts for gas to get to work kek

Early replies (first 3)

61953886
>>61953855
Imagine spending half your days pay worth of money just to get to work and back lmao

61954053
>>61953855
antisemitic wfh antizog antimiga chads will inherit the earth

61954077
>>61953886
>half
You mean full for minimum wage chads, don’t forget about rent and bills too.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61959370
>>61959354
That really depends on the company anon. The easiest is any kind of support desk position, but depending on the company, you could be super busy. Usually, the ones that keep you barely busy will have you do auxiliary shit to make up for your lack of busyness, so you're always going to have some level of work you don't wanna do.

I have 2 WFH jobs because one of my jobs is a lot less involved, they don't monitor you, barely on the phone (mostly emails) but technically I should be doing more (writing articles/making tutorial videos) and I'm just lazy and/or distracted by other job which needs you to be infront of the laptop almost the entire time (phones, emails and chat), hard to get away from it, and they track the shit out of your metrics. I hate it, but the second one is what gives me health insurance and other benefits.

61959379
>>61959331
literally me except no family, damn I'm sad

>>61959354
Being a leader/manager in something. I work in finance and am a low level manager. I started as a nobody on the phones and worked my way to that. I work at home half the time, but I'm gaming 95% of the time when I'm at home. When I'm in a meeting I make sure to not be the presenter that way I can just talk/listen while playing games. The saddest part is I'm still more productive than most people. Us gaming while working is the equivalent of women walking around with StarBucks and talking about nothing all day at work... jk we still do more than they ever would

61959385
>>61953855
AI is going to make everyone return to office.

61959429
>>61959385
???

61959568
>>61958767
go do the work at coffeeshop meme. parks are nice too. my favorite is work from ski lodge

61959577
Well. My wife still goes into jewish daycare every day, so we are still spending money on gas.

61959674
>>61954808
Also accountant here. How easy was it to get a wfh position? What industry? What state?

61959787
>>61954808
Also accountant here. How easy was it to get a wfh position? What industry? What state?

61959961
Anyone got their job recently? Almost landed a wfh gig but after the fourth technical interview they closed the job. It's pretty insane. In the past a phone screen and one interview was all it took. Now you have every humiliation ritual in the book just to get the chance of something

61959986
>>61959961
It’s been that bad for at least ten years. Even before AI, HR was still filtering you with algorithms

61960001
>>61959986
It was way easier in 2021-2024. I had a few remote jobs at once before getting cut back to 1. They let me go due to budget cuts.

61960254
>>61959674
>Also accountant here. How easy was it to get a wfh position? What industry? What state?
>How easy was it to get a WfH position?
I think the job listing wasn't listed as WfH but I ask every interview.
>What industry?
Healthcare management - it's not in healthcare I have several facilities I manage & will be getting more which is why I want a new job
>What state
Sorry don't want to give this

The funny thing is this position asked for like 1-3 years experience but they've taken people fresh out of college and let them WfH
Ideally I want a Gov WfH job but I have no idea if my state even offers remote positions for gov jobs and they take a fucking long time to get back to you, I applied back in December to one and it's still in the pipeline, only 1 update

61960265
>>61960254
I'm a retard
>What industry
It's not in a hospital I meant, I manage several healthcare facilities
Pay is significantly better than what I got paid at my shitty small CPA practice with my cunt post-menopause boss but it's really not a great position

61960308
>>61959961
I'm just applying to everything now, got an assessment with the National Safety Council, they've got a couple different positions open but mostly just looking through support related jobs on LinkedIn. If I nail a $40,000+ a year position that will bring me back to six figures.

61960341
I make 250k a year WFH and I barely do any work. Is anyone else absolutely loving life right now?

61960799
>>61960341
What industry?

61961018
>>61953855
I was WFH today, office wagie tomorrow...
Hybrid bros... Why can't they let us go...

61961897
Implying I don’t dare evade the metro

61962326
>>61960341
im at 120k + benefits and its awesome, can't imagine how nice 250k/year is

what are you investing in?

61962422
>>61959577
The main purpose of female employment is to prevent them from having babies.

61963454
Has anyone here got a WFH in 2025/6? Most seem to be hybrid with full office required during probation

61963700
>>61963454
Yea I snagged a WFH bank job doing cybersecurity end of 2025. Technically we all work at the office but managers tell their teams where to work and my boomer manager is lazy and won't go to the office basically ever so neither does anyone else on his team.

61963769
Anyone else ride an e-bike?

61964574
I have been WFH for two years. For a long time I was playing tons of video games and working maybe 5 hours per week, kek. Now there's a lot more work to do though, but I still only work maybe 20 hours per week on average. The first half of every day I dedicate to reading news, drinking coffee and shitting. I actually feel bad about it as I want to do meaningful work but since we live in clown world I don't care. I was actually the highest performer by far in previous shit jobs like callcenters.

61965044
>>61954585
Came here to post this.

61965065
>>61954976
I just hired someone (WFH) from LinkedIn, I made a post asking for particular skills and he saw it and had a job the next week.
All social media is cancer but LinkedIn does improve work prospects unfortunately.

61965110
>>61964574
I've been WFH for seven years now, a non-coding job watching over a SAAS application. Most days I just fuck around but for example this week I have lots of work. So I fucked around all morning, did nothing during work hours, but now I am bored so I am gonna put in around 4 hours of real work until I feel sleepy. That's probably going to carry me for a couple days.

The flexibility is great its basically like being a full paid NEET if you have a minimum of willpower to actually strategically do work when absolutely needed. I actually get great ratings and raises every year

61965143
WFH healthcare Epic guy here. Have an offer to consult for big bucks, but it will require I leave my home periodically to travel. What do?

61965186
>>61965143
dinners with american boomers is the absolutely most painful part of my WFH job but it hasn't happened in over a year now. I'd say its worth it

61965203
>>61965186
Leaving a full time position for a short contract for the first time scares me, but when you're good (I am) the work keeps coming. Think im going for it.

item.title: OIL TOP CONFIRMED — 58 replies — 232.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
OIL TOP CONFIRMED

Open thread • Replies: 58 • Updated: 2026-03-10 19:42 UTC

OP

WHALE OPENED GIGASHORT ON OIL. EVERYTHING WILL BE FINE AND OIL IS GOING DOWN.

we're good, oil ain't going any lower. buy shorts on monday for free money.

Early replies (first 3)

61953753
>>61953747
wait this was on the 7th. fuck.

61953772
>>61953747
>Entry
>91.431
RIP Bozo. Bobo deserves to lose money.

61953786
I dont short thoughbeit

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61955211
>>61953747
KWABOTY

61955266
top reached

61955307
Looks like he was wrong.

Huh this dumbass thread was only 3 hours ago? Feels longer.

61955510
>>61955266
I just made 60,000 dollars on oil futures and I'm going to have 250k by the end of the week while you keep calling tops that never come.

61955569
>>61955056
/pmg/ here, you are fucking retarded. Do you really think a 2.8M short can have any impact on the largest commodity market in the world lol? Oil is the cornerstone of modern civilization, it's not something you can tank with a fucking 3M bet on binance lol. There is $30M worth of oil futes changing hands every second.

61955988
>>61953944
>Equivalent of 24 future contracts
>Whale
AHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA
You can trade that all in one trade in one contract on one exchange and it wouldn't even register.
Daily Volume in COMEX WTI is like 3 million contracts.
>>61953813
If he gambled 280 million instead of 2.8 he'd move a whopping 0.07% of daily volume. Only on one exchange and one contract, of course.

61955997
>>61953904
They will pay you to store it in a shed you own? I could fit 4 barrels in my tool shed.

61956086
I might throw a little bit into USO, nothing I would get salty about losing if things reversed.

61956162
>>61953747
This one will be wiped out. Always the overly eager bears. Not time yet.

61956284
>if oil pumps to 120/barrel he'll be wiped out

61956296
>>61953747
is that the US government/FED? imagine them getting liquidated, would be funny

61956352
HOLY SHIT LMFAO

INVERSE CRAMER WORKS

HAHAHAHAHA OIL WENT DOWN

61956490
>>61956352
Countries are beginning to unload their oil reserves. If the strait is still closed when they run out then... well...

61956500
Crude tickling the 200ma bounce

61956512
>>61955510
Pressing X

61956748
>>61953747
>3 million
>whale

61959846
>>61956490
Patriots are in command

61960726
>>61953747
Damn, he never misses, does he?

61960738
>>61960726
He definitely is one for the ages

61960749
>>61955510
post loss porn NOW

61962258
>>61953792
>My mom
giga chad, mine tells me the news every morning
t. NEETvestor and successful

61963695
>>61953772
This is the reality you chose

61963773
>>61960749
He's probably killed himself already

61963845
>>61955266
>top reached
Digits and posted 7 minutes before the top

61964410
>>61963773
lmao oil at $77

entire war is a nothingburger

61964732
so everyone is just inside trading at this point? trying to make a quick buck before it all comes tumbling down, without a care whether the npcs catch on or not since they can't do shit anyway? quite diabolical

61964866
>>61953747
>Whale
>Oil
>2,800,000

61964904
>>61953747
>whale
>gigashort
Cryptards are absolute cancer. That's an average 20 contract trade in crude. 200 wouldn't even be close to whale. 2000 maybe.

61965046
>>61953747
>pool still closed, production is still slowing down to avoid getting droned and infrastructure that will take months to years to rebuild is still getting bombed
>Yet the price goes down because the Cheeto's cabinet said the war is already won and reserves (which would at most last for a month) are going to maybe be spent
My fault for not considering market manipulation and vibe investment based on thoughts and prayers.

61965240
>>61965046
Yes, actually. Animal spirits and all that

item.title: >deflation is bad because... uhh... IT JUST IS OKAY?? — 40 replies — 160.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>deflation is bad because... uhh... IT JUST IS OKAY??

Open thread • Replies: 40 • Updated: 2026-03-10 19:47 UTC

OP

>deflation is bad because... uhh... IT JUST IS OKAY??

Early replies (first 3)

61960970
>>61960964
It's bad for rich people

61960978
>>61960964
That student loan debt you selfishly won't pay off will be more difficult to do if we actually went through a deflationary cycle.

61960982
>>61960970
to be more specific, it's bad for the middle class which has most of its wealth in illiquid assets that would implode during deflation

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61961182
>>61960964
The economy runs on juicing shit so if you aren't juicing shit this whole house of cards falls down. We're all leveraged to the gills

61961190
Not this thread again. I will not be participating so someone else is going to need to educate the retards in my absence.

61961199
>>61961118
You're right, they would just become low class and lose all their assets because the middle class is up to their nose in debt and now their cushy middle class do-nothing-job just laid them off because there's a economic constriction.
Whoops now they can't pay their mortgage and they have to sell their house at a loss or get foreclosed on.
Whoops now they can't afford their car payments so they have to sell that at a loss too.
Whoops now there's no jobs on the market because every corpo had to cut jobs to maintain profits from the shrinking economy.

But it's okay! It's actually really good that all those things happened! It's a great thing that all the small businesses got forced to go out of business because they were barely maintaining profit while the economy was expanding, now they lose their business and all their assets and have to file for bankruptcy!
It's a really good thing, I PROMISE!!!


You sound like a literal jew. "JUST EAT THE GOYSLOP IT'S GOOD FOR YOU I PROMISE!!"

61961324
>>61961199
>YOU SOUND LIKE LITERAL JEW FOR NOT WANTING TO BE FORCED TO INVEST ALL YOUR MONEY IN HEEBSOFT INC.

There are different types of deflation useful goyim. There is supply-side deflation (increased productivity caused by technological advancement, economy grows) and demand-side deflation (caused by decreasing demand, economy shrinks.)

Deflation =\= economy HAS to be shrinking. It can just be the buying power of your dollar has increased because efficency has increased supply, which is what naturally happens when society isn't run by a cabal of satanic jewish pedophiles who can print as much monoply money as they please.

61961446
>>61961324
Deflate means to remove from. Deflation is the removal of (money) from (the economy), also known as economic constriction.
Inflate means addition to, addition of money to the economy also known as economic expansion.

Decreasing prices does not mean deflation, you can have increased money (inflation) while prices reduce because the production increases due to the increased economic expansion. Likewise you can reduce the money (deflation) and prices can still rise or stay the same because of the demand and reduced production.

Price deflation is not the same thing as deflation as a general concept. The general concept is that the supply of money has decreased, been deflated. The economic balloon has been deflated and subsequently constricted.
If you use the two terms interchangeably, it makes you sound retarded.

61961465
>>61961190
Somebody buck broke this guy, he's obsessed with his contrarian take

61961769
Same retards telling me Japanese economy is LITERALLY over and going to turn into thailand o algo now tries to tell us deflation is a good thing akshually without realizing the irony

61961778
>>61961048
>people aren't buying things becasue they know most goods will keep getting cheaper
maybe the things are too expensive and people will start buying them when they get cheaper

61961803
>>61961465
It's not even a contrarian take, because that implies there's some chance it's right. Might as well say wanting to jump into lava is a contrarian take

61961808
>>61960964
>>61960964
>>61960964
I AGREE OP. THE (((DEFLATION))) SCAM IS THE BIGGEST SCAM SINCE ZIONISM OR ROCKEFELLER MEDICINE

61961820
Testing to see if I am banned.

Ok continue with this thread. I don't mind. 1/3 of my money is in a savings account for emergencies. As in I don't have to wait a few days to sell anything to get my damn money. More of it is in PMs than stocks. I am covered for just about any scenario. This would only suck for the stock market portfolio.

61961832
>prices go down
yay
>"sorry we're gonna have to cut your pay or let you go due to demand collapse"
HUH

61961833
How about instead of arguing systemics, we focus on the PERSONAL APPEAL for INDIVIDUALS on each side of the argument.
>Deflation
Things i want to buy costs less.
Therefore I can do more things with more things.
>Inflation
Things I already own are now more expensive…
But uhhhhhh I can’t really do anything with them except sit on them, maybe try to buy more to then also sit on.
Idk inflation bros, I might need you to help me out here, the main appeal of inflation kinda seems more like economic paralysis to me….

61961843
>>61961832
>He worked for a company that relied on inflation to keep the lights on.
Skill issue desu sempai.

61961845
>>61961118
retirement is retarded phenomenon we need to return back to mean 3 to 4 generation households as it should be

61961848
>>61961118
>dollar deflates
>I get paid less
>mortgage unchanged

61961877
>>61961848
>Dollar inflates
>FED raises rates to tame inflation.
>Every new guys mortage rates explode.
>(You) feel great until there are literally no buyers for houses under the age of 50 and price destruction starts creeping into your home value anyways. You also had to pay for those higher groceries like everyone else on this entire macroeconomic misadventure.
>[You are here btw]

61961906
>ummmmmm ackshually deflation bad because the economy has turned into a giant ponzi scheme that can only continue because of inflation
>the ponzi should continue because ummmmmm
>the jews should get rich off my back because ummmmmm
>I am mentally retarded because immmmmmmm

61964020
>>61961027
Upper class people are diversified, not everything deflates the same amount at once. They will have the best position when the bottom hits, then they will be the first to benefit from low prices because they can buy immediately while others are recuperating. This exact thing happened in 2008, made triply worse by the bailouts

61964028
>>61960970
But if things cost less why is having less money bad

61964041
>>61961906
>because ummmm
Your strawman can't even bring up the commonly known reason, which is that other countries exist and have no reason or desire to follow a deflationary US. The first country to deflate is going to be the one that gets fucked the hardest by it, overnight manufacturing will shit the bed and exports will fall off a cliff. It's a big mexican standoff between the US, Russia, China, and Europe. Japan has had a 'deflationary' economy, the only reason it's still afloat is because the government is printing like madmen the keep a reasonable currency parity to the USD

61964146
The great depression was deflation

61964580
>>61964146
Only due to a previous government created inflation

The fucking industrial revolution in America was a deflationary period for decades

61964593
>>61960982
If deflation is bad for the middle class why did all the decades of inflation extinguish it? Surely it should have increased it if this was true. I feel like I'm going crazy here but is applying rational thinking antisemitic? As a goy I feel like dealing in the real world truly is for me.

61964827
>>61964028
That is if everything deflates equally. If that happens then deflation is great because then everyone’s cash savings increase in purchasing power.

But what really happens is investment assets crash much harder then dollar devaluates so people with assets still experience a big net loss. While cash chads get a generational buying opportunity. Really great for young people without much assets

61964930
>>61964827
in a deflationary economy there is no investment as we can imagine it in our current economy. people who work just work and won't invest, that would be a job on its own.

61965076
>>61964593
The best economy is with almost zero inflation or deflation. In order to achieve this money is only printed at a rate equal to the creation of new value within an economy, so even though total amount of circulating cash goes up prices stay the same because as more value is created more cash (units of value) is needed to compensate for trade between the people, otherwise in this example of a surplus economy if we didn’t print any money at all it would result in a deflationary economy which comes with it’s own set of problems.

Inflation can also happen with zero money printing also, for example an economy that has more destruction of value vs value creation there then becomes more circulating cash relative to total amount of asset value in a national resulting in inflation.

t. Economics PHD

61965099
>>61965076
You should get a refund for that PhD because smol inflation is ideal

61965130
>>61960964
Supply side deflation is not a problem. Deflation fear mongering is gay AF.

61965269
>>61965099
I wouldn’t want to encourage more spending that way. Even at 1% a year, it is still a transfer of wealth from people that hold cash to asset holders. A proper society doesn’t devalue its own currency to get people to invest in things out of inflationary fears. Having an economy worth investing in is what will encourage people to invest. There is no need for any modification whatsoever to keeping things properly balanced, any modifications is a sign your economy is run by Jews

item.title: There are now more tech workers going from the US to Europe than the other way around — 45 replies — 94.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
There are now more tech workers going from the US to Europe than the other way around

Open thread • Replies: 45 • Updated: 2026-03-10 19:21 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

61952823
>>61952797
is this another one of those tricks where India becomes Southern Asian becomes Eastern Europe?

61952844
>>61952797
Ok, but why are both decreasing?

61952879
>>61952844
it looks to me that there are less Tech workers in general, the ones going back to Europe probably couldn't get a job in the US

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61956746
>>61952797
that's h1b people getting kicked out of US
>>61956671
every cunt in EU was conquered like 50 times in history

61956786
>>61956746
I mean europe as a whole.

61956789
>>61956668
The number of private tech start ups valued above $1bn in Europe has gone up from ~15 to ~300 in about 10 years time.

61956794
>>61956786
Name 1 continent that was ever conquered as a whole. The fuck kind of retarded goalpost is that

61956838
>>61952797
The amount of mutt coping in this thread is absolutely hilarious. These brown subhumans never learn.

61956843
>>61956838
It's really not Americans, anon. It's majority thirdies (arabs, russians).
Probably some mutts as well, but they are mostly asleep right now.

61956849
>>61956794
The idea is that the goal of europeans is to not be conquered by foreign powers right now, retard.

61956857
>>61956664
This is cultural. Americans are encouraged to spend 115% of their income while European culture values security thru saving.
>>61952797
I am a U.S. tech worker in Europe. It isn't bad if you already have money from America to buy a house in Europe. I get literally over 2 months off per year between all the PTO and holidays. Pay is about 1/3 what I made in America but it's WFH at least and no niggers at all near me.

61956859
>>61956857
>no niggers at all near me
Don't say that in 4chan. Anons will call you a liar and tell Europe is majority African.

61956861
>>61956668
Spotify
Revolut
Various other fintech companies
Video game companies are doing well (except ubisoft)

61956865
Oh yea the other big deal in European tech is the desire to move away from U.S. based tech.
This will drive a huge boom in E.U. tech and coding jobs for 15+ years.
Americans really underestimate how petty and spiteful Europeans are. The people I work with don't even want to use U.S. clouds or Windows at all.

61956870
>>61956865
Any ways you can think to profit off of Linux? I feel it is finally reaching normal people, irregardless of Europeans

61956885
>>61952797
>Europoor cope.
Just ask an AI what the economic outlook is for the next 10 years for the US. Then for any Europoor country.

61956888
>>61956870
Learn linux.
Charge companies $$$ to install open source systems and maintain them.
Remember, you are charging them for the labor and not the software itself.

61956914
>>61956870
>>61956888
German government is again trying to switch their enterprise environments to Linux from MS.
So yea, but you need to speak German. But in general there is an uptick in E.U. demand for Linux experts...

61956916
>>61956885
>just ask the jewish USA produced chat bots what the jews programmed them to say
Nah. But they vary wildly week to week anyway. Grok is usually pro-USA but lately admits the only advantage to the USA is money at higher personal risk to crime and violence and imprisonment etc.

61956926
>>61956865
>oh hey we’re going to annex a huge territory belonging to an european nation while insulting in white house the leader of another european nation that is currently under threat of annexation in a war
>wtf why are they not trusting us anymore?? What spiteful creatures!

The mind of a burger is truly an interesting one.

61956941
>>61952901
europeans cant afford to save but do
americans cant afford to spend but do

61956988
>>61956926
You have to understand Amero-Jewish culture. It's not personal, it's just business. America's vicious jewish culture of fucking people over and jewing them out of their money and property doesn't allow for holding grudges.
Anyway, yea Europeans have diverged massively from Americans and that is the basic root of this schism and it is pathetic to watch Mexico Rubio come grovel to the Europeans and be like "hey guys we're still cool right? Our shared history and Christianity." Mesnwhile he's a spic from Cuba and 90% of Europeans are secular atheists.

61959430
>>61956988
Lol such a true post it ended the thread.

61959479
>>61956988
These fucking rural schizo yahoos like Vance coming here to lecture us boils my blood like nothing else

61959509
>>61956786
>he doesn't know...

61959529
>>61952797
Well, having nearly 10 weeks of comfy yearly paid leave does that

61959554
>>61956849
>the goal
Yeah that is the goal, but you didn't say it's the goal you said Europe is "unconquered" which is obviously not true, retard

61960952
>>61959479
That's your European elitism and snobbishness. In America white trash garbage like Vance can rise to such power that he can be richer and more powerful than any European even to the point your aristocratic old money elites have to listen to him call you bitch ass faggots to your faces.

61960963
>>61952797
Makes sense. Usa has no growth. Now Europe will have growth.

61961283
>>61959529
Coulda just been a teacher for all of those benefits

61961513
Local Europeans are delusional, hubris awaits them.

61965098
>>61952797
And all of them are Indians.

61965105
>>61956794
Australia

item.title: How would you breakout of the NEET cycle if you had too — 39 replies — 89.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
How would you breakout of the NEET cycle if you had too

Open thread • Replies: 39 • Updated: 2026-03-10 19:23 UTC

OP

How would you breakout of the NEET cycle if you had too

Early replies (first 3)

61956383
>>61956364
Stop dealing in the real life.

61956387
By cumming all over OP pic

61956426
>>61956364
I'd EET

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61956702
>>61956436
How is an online degree going to help OP who likely has a CS degree from an actual university?

61956707
>>61956702
Where did OP mention that? He likely has 0 degree and low money. Post-covid online degrees are normalized esp in tech

61956723
>>61956707
Because it's 4chan and most 4chan NEETs have degrees?
You don't go from applying to hundreds of minimum wage jobs and not getting a single interview to suddenly landing interviews and getting jobs just because you've done a degree
That's not how it works
You're just setting OP up to more disappointment

61956731
>>61956723
I dint give advice on applying for jobs because it depends on what he studies. But absolutely getting a degree and verified skills moves the ball for literally anyone. You’re saying self-defeating shit

61957085
>>61956364
There's no magical solution. Your question is shallow and pointless without giving us context. I assume:
>you are living with your parents
>you don't have a car (and probably no license)
>you are nearing 30 or even reached it already
>you don't have a degree
>you have a high school diploma but nothing remarkable, judging from the way you ask questions expecting everything to be chewed and spoonfed to you, and from your typos
>probably have some sort of a degeneracy that is time and money consuming
It would really help if you could write down your situation and not just vaguepost like a retard.
Also, you decided to get out of NEETdom literally at the worst possible time since the covid lockdowns. Good luck with that, but here are my tips:
First and foremost, you need to unfuck yourself. You need to get used to suffer for long hours. You need to get used to a situation where there's no light at the end of the tunnel. Laziness is fucking over - you wake up and it is all suffering until one hour before sleep. There's no comfort anymore in your world. Can't deal with this? Give up already and stay a decaying corpse.
After you are sure you are resilient and ready to make it, do the following:
>stay with your parents no matter what. you will have to still leech on them for the next several years if not decade
>get a job, even if it is the most basic shit, deliver food for all I care
>get a car license if you don't have one
>get a beater reliable car - rare these days but possible
>work on a degree that will be useful in the near future, try to aim for becoming a nurse because that's a guaranteed job forever
>get a job where you can save money
When you reach this point, make another post asking what's next.

61957118
>>61956364
personally I'd register an entity with SAM, search upcoming contracting opportunities on SAM, find a janitorial contract in my area on SAM, find out how much it currently pays by searching past awards on SAM, find the Contracting Officer on SAM, and then contact them with my entity name and email on SAM and tell them I'm interested in bidding.

Then I'd bid the job lower than it's currently being paid and when I got the job I'd go clean a government building 6 days per week for hundreds of dollars per hour.

then I'd repeat that process 5 or 6 more times and start hiring other losers to work for me cleaning government buildings for hundreds of dollars per hour.

Then I'd hire one more loser to watch all those losers, and then another loser to find jobs and bid them, and then I'd retire.

61957123
>>61956426
TRVKE

61957212
>>61956364
deport all indians

61957756
>>61956364
I'm not sure how I'd lie about a 14 year gap.

61957816
>>61956364

61957838
You have to put barriers between yourself and your enabler

If its your parents it might mean moving away and changing your number, if its your government then idk

61957974
>>61957085
>just live a horrible life with no way out for 10 years instead of just being a comfy neet

wagies are incredible

61958365
>>61957974
I used to be a NEET. Yes, live life horribly for a few years to guarantee stability and a future. If you can NEET until death then by all means go for it. I personally was running out of time, and I'm the type of person that wants to start a family so NEETing is not a real option (it could be an option if I was genetically blessed enough for a rich girl to keep me as a sex-toy stay-at-home husband, but alas my dick is only 6 inches and I'm a 6ft king of manlets)

61958461
>>61958365

Uhh anon... manlet cutoff was officially extended to 6'2" in January 2025...

61958498
>>61958365

61962025
>>61956662

61962105
>>61958365
How is not neeting help you with starting a family? You will have more time picking up women and going on a date as a neet.
Once you find a chick you want to start a family with, you should stop neeting.
You got it backwards.

61962123
>>61956364
Join the army. Which is what I did. Now im out and i literally ended up back where I started and neeting again. Life's terrible

61962255
Impregnate an 18 year old redhead

Wait, what were we talking about??

61962677
>>61962123
Now probably isn't the best time to join the army

61962712
>>61956364
Abby Champion for the wonderers. She is married to a negger

61962739
>>61956364
i can tell you how I did it:
get the simplest, no burden job available. can be part time
initially it will be very anxiety filled, but you'll get used to it in a few months.
and everyone will prise you, even though you're working half time, because even that is better than rotting aimlessly in your parents basement.
with time you can switch to something more ambitious

Save everything you make, dont spend it on frivolities. You can spend your new money on things that will make you more money. a lot of it depends on how forgiving your parents are, but since you''re already a neet, they're probably pretty forgiving.

61962762
>>61956364>>61956364
be an uber driver bro, go out at 2:00 am and girls that didn't get laid will wanna fuck you..i picked up a passenger, it was 2 in the morning, she was so horny. we parked on the side of the house and fcuked for an hour, we were sweating so much the windows were completely drenched in condensation, but i couldn't roll down the windows because she was so loud moaning, i had to cover her mouth forcefully with my hands. while i was banging her, she was cumming so so much. i probably gave her about 5 orgasms. she was begging for me to cum inside of her, she wanted my cum. i could taste cocaine on her saliva while making out. i had to wait about 15 minutes before i could drive again because the windows were not drying with the defrosters. they were that drenched. she only left a 5 dollar tip.i was dehydrated and she bit my lip that still hurts two days later. she told me that it had been so long since she last had sex. and she was very very beautiful. beautiful tits. perfect bubble ass, sexy panties. uber really is the best job, i get paid to pick up hot women and fuck them, her pussy was freshly shaven... i know for sure she shaved it only hours before... she was looking to get fcuked that night ... she was wearing a very sexy dress , i couldn't believe it. i know she was just so horny
she asked if i had a gf and i said yea a couple.... she said she doesn't like to share i said yea i know... then she laughed
pic related was after we were having sex. her hair was all sweaty as was mine. and she still wanted more. she couldnt keep her hands off me. she was licking my lips in pic related.

girl was gorgeous to she looked like alt goth megan fox. and like that olympic gold medal girl.

61962795
>>61956364
Getting a job I enjoy and a finding a cute wife that sticks with me. I've been trying for the past two years without much success unfortunately.

I don't know what could make my luck work from me again, it wasn't that bad before.

61963129
>>61956364
This is some shitty engagement bait thread but I'll bite.
Join the military.
Your training will be with young retards but your actual job will be with people your age.

61963202
>>61956364
Change all of your habits in one go, like an addict trying to recover. Imagine you are an actor playing a character, try and stay in character all day. The character is the person you want to be. Since you are pretending to be that person, your actions and speech will mirror that person. That will create new neural pathways and become a habit. You will slowly become that person and no longer “act”, it will be natural.

The first steps are the hardest. Move cities, move jobs, change friends. You need to completely 180 your life.

61963205
>>61963129
Bad time to do it due to Iran bullshit but unironically good advice if you are a neet.

61964254
>>61962762

61965045

61965119

item.title: The "secret" to "making it" is legitimately just saving up and investing long term in Gold/index funds no matter what. — 4 replies — 16.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The "secret" to "making it" is legitimately just saving up and investing long term in Gold/index funds no matter what.

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-03-10 19:39 UTC

OP

The "secret" to "making it" is legitimately just saving up and investing long term in Gold/index funds no matter what. This sounds like typical Boomer advice until you realize the "no matter what" here is carrying A LOT of weight.

I mean straight purging your friend groups that pressure you into spending, struggle foods galore, maybe working 2 jobs, never sending gifts, live in a shack/pod, never buying a car, ditching your culture/family and so on.

Just make the numbers make sense where in 15 years, you are where you went to be. Not everyone can handle it and not everyone should do it. But that's kind of the point in why it works.

61960960
>>61958261
>Why does he write like an absolute retard, can Americans explain this to me?
DJT doesn't speak english. He speaks english words, but not grammar, syntax, or meaning.

he spouts vague chain of consciousness word salad, which other people are free to interpret according to their own epistemic lens. This makes him everyone to everybody, and also results in success usually since smarter people than him take his nonsense and filter it into actionable concepts that yield results.

as to whether or not he's actually retarded, we will never know. He doesn't speak english, so it's impossible to judge his intellect. Though the simple failure to learn language is itself a strong sign of retardation. So It's likely he speaks like a retard simply because he is a retard.

>Please leave your political posts at the door unless they involve Business or Finance.

>Educational sites:

https://market24hclock.com/

61962136
>>61962128
>WTI 89
>Brent 93

can someone explain why?

61962139
>>61962128
the us treasury staff is asleep so they can't suppress now

61962141
>>61962136
wti and brent are different. wti comes from texas and it is used to supply the american market. brent is heavier and it's what's in the oilfields in the middle east, north sea, other areas, it's used to make diesel fuel primarily and it's what the rest of the world's supply chains and refineries are built to handle. it has a premium because of the geopolitical risk and higher utilization.

61962145
>>61962128
Don’t give me false hope

61962146
>>61962136
They know that GAYS like to feel as if they are buying nice premium designer luxury things.

61962148
>futures

61962153
>>61962128
Would be kino and very profitable if Iran blew up a tanker just in time for me to read morning news. ;-)

61962154

long long long (oil)

61962155
>>61962154

61962156
>>61962141

61965107

item.title: What if BTC (and others) become fuel for vehicles? — 6 replies — 15.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
What if BTC (and others) become fuel for vehicles?

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-03-10 19:25 UTC

OP

What if BTC (and others) become fuel for vehicles? Not as a literal fuel where the BTC is consumed to power it, but that BTC acts as the medium for energy exchange.
If you don't have BTC you can't drive because all energy is traded with crypto. As long as there's crypto in the wallet attached to your car, you never run out of energy. You don't stop at a pump to refill, your SATs just decrease as you exchange them for energy to drive.

Sort of like mining in reverse, except the BTC isn't lost, it's just traded for the value of energy.
Or it could be burned and returned to the unrealized supply to be re-mined.

Early replies (first 3)

61964780
>>61964756
this is how it will work when the orbital solar panels are online beaming energy back down to the earth. if you default, the beam just fries you instead of powering your engine

61964790
>>61964756
Why would anyone want this?

61964808
>>61964790
Take money power away from states and (((financiers)))?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61964824
>>61964780
>if you default, the beam just fries you instead of powering your engine
That's actually fucking hilarious. It'd be like that movie where everyone has a timer on their wrist and if it hits zero they die. Except it's BTC and if you run out of SATs you die.
10/10 dystopian world, would invest.
>>61964790
Because of the way BTC is created/mined. It's a measure of the value of energy used to create it.
It's the same principle that made gold valuable as a coin. It took manpower to extract and refine, therefore you could trade it for manpower to do some other job.

61964899
>>61964808
Forcing a bunch of arbitrary shitcoin market rules onto every car owner only sounds like taking money and power away from the owner of the car.

>>61964824
>Because of the way BTC is created/mined. It's a measure of the value of energy used to create it.
No it isn't. Pic related.
>It's the same principle that made gold valuable as a coin. It took manpower to extract and refine, therefore you could trade it for manpower to do some other job.
That's not at all how the gold price has been determined throughout the last several thousand years. Not even the fucking gold mining companies themselves adhere to that sort of marxist bullshit theory.

61965131
>>61964899
Your pic does nothing, it states nothing and explains nothing.

Just because it's related to Marx' labor theory of value, doesn't mean it's Marxist, you retarded ass doofus ass stupid ass retarded ass dingle ass retard.
It's just a very simple capitalist economic principle. It's the very exact principle that has determined the value of gold and everything else since forever. It's the very same reason we don't use globs of mud as trading currency. It has little value due to the fact it takes very little effort to obtain, it takes very little labor and very little time and very little energy, on top of that it's abundant.

Let's pretend for a moment that it's very early in the advent of society and I'm building a house for you. If I'm to build this house, you need to give me something of equal or greater value to the time and effort I'm putting into your house + supplies. But, we'll assume you have all the supplies, you just need labor.
Let's say it takes a week for me to build it. You need to pay me one week of time+labor as currency. Is one mud glob worth a week? No, it only takes a very seconds to gather and pour water on.
But let's say it takes 1 month to gather 1oz of gold, turn that oz into 16 1gram coins, then you offer me 4 of those coins for the house I built you. This is called fair trade. The labor + time value of your 4 coins is equal to, or greater than the value of my labor + time to build your house. Especially when everyone agrees that gold coins is the measure of value.

The same principle can be applied to BTC as a value of energy. Because energy + computational power is what created BTCs. If there's no miners, there's no BTC. It literally cannot be traded without miners.

item.title: Housing Crash Delayed yet again — 20 replies — 8.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Housing Crash Delayed yet again

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-03-10 17:33 UTC

OP

Uh oh Rentoids!
>US Existing Home Sales Bounced In February As Mortgage Rates Tumbled
>Consensus was for a modest 0.8% MoM decline in February (again despite an ongoing drop in mortgage rates) but sales actually surprised to the upside, rising 1.7% MoM.
>with mortgage rates at their lowest since 2022, existing home sales look set to continue to improve
>The NAR report showed the median selling price rose 0.3% from a year earlier
>Market analysts see home sales climbing this year, with estimates ranging from 1.7% to 14%, according to a survey by Bloomberg late last year.

But yeah, that housing market crash is coming any day now, keep renting until then! Just two more weeks!

Early replies (first 3)

61963664
Fuck you I will get a home for 4 ounces of silver soon and you will beg me for it

61963697
getting ready to buy my 2nd rental
you guys can go on about the inevitable crash
rent is due 1st of the month

61963806
Housing will just keep going up and it’s not gonna stop, if you are priced out now then you are priced out forever
There will be no housing crash, most homeowners are cash positive on their mortgages unlike the 2008 crash

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61963886
>>61963604
>>61963664
>>61963697
>>61963806

MODS! the bots are getting real good, I'm fucking scared!

These threads are always silly to me, I've rented and now I'm a Landch@d myself. DESU the difference between me and the peeps I rent to is minimal.

Always give people a good deal for the current situation and they live a little easier. Listen to them vent a bit and they give plenty of grace to fix a repair problem(Mostly small ones)

Bad rentoids get the fucking boot quick tho

61963890
>>61963604

61963917

61964300
